records.ety: [OE. lese, lees, leece, OF. lesse, F. laisse, LL. laxa, fr. L. laxus loose. See Lax.]

defs: A thong of leather, or a long cord, by which a person may hold or restrain an animal, such as a falconer holding his hawk, or a courser his dog. For dogs and cats, the leash is commonly attached to a collar around the neck of the animal.
quotes: Even like a fawning greyhound in the leash.

defs: A brace and a half; a tierce; three; three creatures of any kind, especially greyhounds, foxes, bucks, and hares; hence, the number three in general.
quotes: [I] kept my chamber a leash of days.
Then were I wealthier than a leash of kings.

defs: A string with a loop at the end for lifting warp threads, in a loom.

records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Leashed (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Leashing.]

defs: To tie together, or hold, with a leash.
